content.full_text:
November 30, 1977                                    A-77-11-034

Mr. Paul J. Kelley
Treasurer
U-Haul Political Action Committee
P.O. Box 21510
Phoenix, Arizona  85036

Dear Mr. Kelley:

Your letter addressed to the Secretary of State's office dated
November 11, 1977, has been forwarded to the Fair Political
Practices Commission for clarification of the U-Haul Political
Action Committee's filing obligation under the Political Reform
Act of 1974.

Pursuant to Section 82013 of the Act, a committee is defined as
"any person or combination of persons who directly or indirectly
receives contributions or makes expenditures or contributions for
the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action
of the voters for or against the nomination or election of one or
more candidates, or the passage or defeat of any measure, including
any committee or subcommittee of a political party, whether
national, state or local, if: (a) Contributions received total
five hundred dollars ($500) or more in a calendar year; (b) Expen-
ditures and contributions made, other than contributions described
in subsection (c), total five hundred dollars ($500) or more in a
calendar year; or (c) Contributions of cash, checks and other cash
equivalents paid directly to candidates and committees total
five thousand dollars ($5,000) or more in a calendar year...."

If your committee collected contributions and/or made expenditures
of $500 or more during the period covered (January 1 - December 31,
1976) on the Major Donor Statement you filed with the Secretary of
State's office, for the purpose of making political contributions,
it qualified as a "Recipient Committee" (type d) and should have
filed statements in connection with the election.  Because it
continued to make contributions, it also should have filed one off-
year report during the period when an election was not being held.
The periods for filing an off-year statement are January 1 (or the
day after the closing date of the last statement filed) through
June 30 (due July 31) and July 1 (or the day after the closing date
of the last statement filed) through December 31 (due January 31).

17

Mr. Paul J. Kelley                    -2-                    November 30, 1977


     If it is determined the political action committee is a "Recipient
Committee" it should file an original and one copy of the Statement
of Organization (Form 410) with the Secretary of State's office and
file amended campaign statements with the appropriate filing
officers.  The enclosed Form 420 should be used if the committee
received cumulative contributions of $50 or more from a single
source; or Form 450, if no contributions of $50 or more were
received from a single source.

     I have enclosed some material that will be of assistance to you and
have added your name to our mailing list to receive the revised
campaign disclosure manual which will be available January 1.

     I hope the above answers your question.  If I may be of further
assistance, do not hesitate to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Mary Ann Kvasager
                                        Technical Assistance and
                                        Analysis Division

mak
Enclosures

.  .
. .

[SEAL: Great Seal of California]                                           [POLITICAL REFORM DIVISION
                                                                            (916) 322-4880
                Office of the Secretary of State     111 Capitol Mall, Rm. 200
                March [illegible]                    P.O. Box 1467
                                                     Sacramento, California 95807


November 16, 1977


Mr. Paul J. Kelley
Treasurer
U-Haul International
P.O. Box 21510
Phoenix, AZ  85036

Dear Mr. Kelley:

Thank you for your letter of November 19 wherein you indicate you
have not received a response to your letter of January 14, 1977.

Apparently because of the information contained in the document filed
and the statement in your letter indicating that you had used the
"Interim Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of
Proposition Nine," we did not recognize your request for additional
information or guidelines. I apologize for this oversight.

Based on the information provided on the Major Donor Campaign State-
ment, this office construed that filing to be voluntary in that your
total contributions made during 1976 did not appear to exceed $5,000.

In order to clarify your duties under the California Political Reform
Act of 1974, we are providing a copy of your letters, Major Donor
Statement, and this letter to the Fair Political Practices Commission
so they can contact you, ascertain any additional information, and
advise you of your past and future reporting duties under the Act.

If you have any questions pertaining to this matter, please feel free
to contact this office. However, if your questions are of an inter-
pretive nature, I recommend you contact the Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission directly
at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

WILLIAM N. DURLEY
Assistant to the Secretary of State
Elections and Political Reform

        [signature]
DAVID B. PITMAN, Manager
Political Reform Division

Enclosures

cc:  Fair Political Practices Commission

U-HAUL INTERNATIONAL / 2727 NORTH CENTRAL AVENUE • P.O. BOX 21510, PHOENIX, ARIZONA 85036 • (602) 263-6011

11 November 1977

Secretary of State
State of California
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, Calif. 95807

Dear Sir:

On January 14, 1977 I filed a statement regarding campaign
contributions as I understood the California Disclosure Law on
this subject. In that letter I requested additional information
and/or guidance regarding my future responsibilities under this
law.

I never received a response to that letter.

With the close of 1977 approaching, I again assume I must file
some type of statement of campaign contributions. Please send me
the appropriate forms, information and instruction sheets, and the
applicable regulations. Thank you very much.

Sincerely,

Paul J. Kelley
Chairman
U-Haul Political Action Committee
U-PAC

PJK:ms

A SERVICE GROUP OF AMERCO, INC.

U-HAUL
HOUSEHOLD MOVING

U-HAUL INTERNATIONAL/2727 NORTH CENTRAL AVENUE / P.O. BOX 21510, PHOENIX, ARIZONA 85036 • (502) 263-6011

January 14, 1977

Secretary of State
State of California
P. O. Box 1467
Sacramento, CA 95807

Dear Sir:

Using the "Interim Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure
Provisions of Proposition Nine" and "Form 440" sent to me by your
office, I was under the belief that groups making contributions
totalling under $5,000 each year did not have to file with your
office. Recently I was informed that such a belief is incorrect.
Therefore, I belatedly and apologetically enclose such a report.

Also, the expenditures made were not (with one exception, being the
$250 contribution to Citizens for Maxine Waters) to campaign
committees prior to an election, but to "testimonial/appreciation"-
type committees honoring a city official. I do not know whether
such contributions must be reported, but I am doing so to make
this late report as complete as possible.

Thank you for your interest and understanding. I would most
appreciate any additional information and/or guidance you can
provide me concerning future responsibilities.

Sincerely,

Paul J. Kelley
Treasurer
U-HAUL POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE

cc: Mel Lewis, President, U-Haul Co. of Los Angeles
    Bill Huenemeier, President, U-Haul Co. of Van Nuys
    Jeff Wilson, President, U-Haul Co. of San Bernardino

A MOVING SERVICE COMPANY OF AMERICA ©

Form 440

(Interim Form)
MAJOR DONOR'S
CAMPAIGN STATEMENT

(Persons making contributions totaling $5,000 or more in a
calendar year must file this statement. Persons who
receive contributions or make expenditures may file Form 420.)

GOVERNMENT CODE SECTIONS 84200-84214

Statement covers period Jan. 1 through Dec. 31

FOR OFFICIAL

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

*If committee does not have I.D. Number, list full name and address of treasurer

YOU MUST COMPLETE THE SUMMARY AND VERIFICATION ON PAGE 2

ONLY C D E F

V     SUMMARY

6. CUMULATIVE CONTRIBUTIONS MADE FROM PRIOR PERIOD                                  $

_____________________________________________________________________

VERIFICATION

I have not received any contributions during this calendar year.

I have not made expenditures except contributions of cash, checks and other cash equivalents paid directly to candidates and committees supporting candidates or ballot measures.

I declare under penalty of perjury that this campaign statement is true, correct and complete to the best of my knowledge and I have used all reasonable diligence in its preparation.

Executed on __Jan 14, 1977__ at __Phoenix, AZ__ by _________________ 
                    (DATE)                    (CITY AND STATE)                    (SIGNATURE)

sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing obligations for U-Haul Political Action Committee under the Political Reform Act of 1974 given its 1976 contribution activity, and what forms and procedures must be followed?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: U-Haul Political Action Committee qualifies as a 'Recipient Committee' and must file a Statement of Organization (Form 410) and amended campaign statements (Forms 420 or 450) with appropriate filing officers, plus off-year reports when elections are not being held.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple related letters: initial request to Secretary of State (11 Nov 1977, 19 Nov date reference), SOS response (16 Nov 1977), and original PAC letter with Major Donor Statement (14 Jan 1977). FPPC response letter is from 30 Nov 1977 by Mary Ann Kvasager. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections with headers, but content clearly establishes the legal question and FPPC's definitive answer regarding committee classification and filing requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the filing obligations for U-Haul Political Action Committee under the Political Reform Act of 1974 given its 1976 contribution activity, and what forms and procedures must be followed?

CONCLUSION: U-Haul Political Action Committee qualifies as a 'Recipient Committee' and must file a Statement of Organization (Form 410) and amended campaign statements (Forms 420 or 450) with appropriate filing officers, plus off-year reports when elections are not being held.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to Paul J. Kelley of U-Haul Political Action Committee regarding filing obligations under California's Political Reform Act of 1974. The FPPC clarifies that the committee's contributions of approximately $4,950 in 1976 qualify it as a Recipient Committee requiring formal registration and disclosure statement filings.
